title: PhD Scholarship Opportunity: Future Rural Africa Project (Subproject B03 “Violent Futures”) in Tanzania & Kenya – Fully Funded Ethnographic Research Position
summary: The Future Rural Africa Project is inviting applications for a highly competitive PhD scholarship under its Subproject B03 titled “Violent Futures.” This research-focused doctoral opportunity is designed for scholars interested in understanding how emerging carbon credit initiatives influence social relations, conflict dynamics, and future-making processes in rural African contexts, particularly in Tanzania and Kenya . This is a long-term, field-intensive academic opportunity ideal for candidates with strong interests in anthropology, political ecology, development studies, and conflict research. The project places special emphasis on ethnographic fieldwork , meaning selected candidates will engage in deep, immersive research within communities affected by carbon credit and climate-related development initiatives. About the Research Project: “Violent Futures” The Subproject B03 “Violent Futures” investigates the complex relationship between future-oriented carbon credit schemes and their real-world impacts on social structures, land use, and conflict formation in East Africa. Key Research Focus Areas The PhD candidate will contribute to understanding: Carbon Credit Projects and Social Transformation How carbon credit and climate mitigation initiatives reshape rural economies The role of global climate markets in local governance systems Conflict and Social Relations Emerging tensions between communities, institutions, and investors Land access disputes linked to environmental and conservation policies Ethnographic Fieldwork in Tanzania and Kenya Long-term immersion in rural communities Qualitative data collection including interviews, participant observation, and field notes Future-Making and Environmental Governance How communities imagine and negotiate their futures under climate interventions Power relations between local actors and international climate finance structures Scholarship Details This PhD scholarship offers a structured research environment with financial support for the full duration of the program. Key Facts Duration: 41 months Monthly stipend: €1,475 Start date: Ideally 1 August 2026 Application deadline: 21 June 2026 What the Opportunity Offers This PhD position provides a unique combination of academic training, field experience, and financial support. Key benefits include: Long-term funded doctoral research Access to a major interdisciplinary research project Opportunity to conduct fieldwork in Tanzania and Kenya Engagement with global debates on climate justice and carbon markets Development of advanced ethnographic and qualitative research skills Collaboration with international researchers and institutions Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by: 21 June 2026 Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early. Ideal Candidate Profile This opportunity is best suited for candidates who demonstrate: Strong academic background in social sciences or related disciplines Interest in anthropology, political ecology, environmental studies, or development studies Experience or strong willingness to conduct ethnographic fieldwork Analytical interest in climate change, governance, and conflict studies Ability to work independently in field environments in East Africa Strong writing and research skills at postgraduate level Application Link Click here for more and to apply For more global scholarship opportunities, Click here The post PhD Scholarship Opportunity: Future Rural Africa Project (Subproject B03 “Violent Futures”) in Tanzania Kenya – Fully Funded Ethnographic Research Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027: Fully Funded Nursing Doctorate in the USA for Future Nurse Scientists, Educators, Researchers, and Policy Leaders
summary: The Boston College Connell School of Nursing (CSON) is inviting applications for its prestigious Ph.D. Program in Nursing, a fully funded doctoral program designed to prepare the next generation of nurse scientists, educators, researchers, and healthcare policy leaders. Located at Boston College in Massachusetts, United States, the program combines advanced scientific inquiry, nursing theory, and rigorous research training to equip scholars with the knowledge and expertise needed to transform healthcare systems and improve patient outcomes globally. One of the most attractive features of the program is its comprehensive financial support package. The Connell School of Nursing fully funds tuition, fees, and health insurance for all full-time Ph.D. students during their first three years of study, making it one of the most competitive nursing doctoral opportunities available. With a strong emphasis on research excellence, leadership development, and interdisciplinary collaboration, graduates emerge prepared to influence healthcare policy, conduct groundbreaking nursing research, educate future healthcare professionals, and lead innovation across healthcare institutions worldwide. About the Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program The Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program integrates science, theory, and research methodologies to prepare highly skilled nurse scientists capable of addressing complex healthcare challenges. The program attracts students from diverse academic, clinical, and professional backgrounds who share a commitment to advancing nursing science and improving healthcare delivery. Students work closely with distinguished faculty members whose expertise spans numerous healthcare and nursing disciplines. Throughout their doctoral journey, candidates receive mentorship, research guidance, and professional development opportunities designed to support successful academic and professional careers. Program Highlights Fully Funded Doctoral Education Every full-time Ph.D. student receives: Full tuition coverage Mandatory fees coverage Health insurance coverage Financial support for the first three years of study This funding package significantly reduces financial barriers and allows students to focus fully on their academic and research pursuits. Duration of Study Students generally complete the Ph.D. program within four years. Program Start Date The program begins each Fall semester. Academic Structure Most students complete: 20 courses 46 total academic credits The curriculum is designed to progressively develop research expertise, analytical thinking, and advanced nursing scholarship. Curriculum Overview The doctoral curriculum combines foundational nursing science with advanced research methods and statistical analysis. First-Year Core Courses Fall Semester Evidence Appraisal and Synthesis Philosophical Inquiry for Knowledge Development in Nursing Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis Survey of Research Methods in Social and Behavioral Science Spring Semester Strategies for Knowledge Development Advanced Qualitative Research Methods Regression Analysis for Social and Behavioral Science These courses establish a strong foundation in research design, statistical analysis, evidence-based practice, and theoretical development. Research Opportunities A major strength of the Connell School Ph.D. Program is its extensive focus on research. Students actively participate in scholarly activities and contribute to studies addressing critical healthcare challenges such as: Public health and health systems Chronic disease management Mental health Healthcare quality and safety Nursing ethics Genomic healthcare disparities Substance use and chronic pain management Doctoral candidates regularly present their findings at national and international conferences and publish in respected academic journals. Many students also receive prestigious honors and recognition, including induction into the American Academy of Nursing and selection as Jonas Center for Nursing Excellence Scholars. Distinguished Faculty Mentorship Students benefit from mentorship by internationally recognized nursing scholars and researchers. Faculty members guide students through: Research development Dissertation planning Publication strategies Career preparation Academic leadership development The school s supportive academic culture helps students build confidence and expertise throughout the doctoral journey. Career Opportunities After Graduation Graduates of the Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program pursue leadership positions across healthcare, academia, research institutions, and government agencies. Recent graduates have secured positions at: American Nurses Foundation Baylor College of Medicine Boston Children s Hospital Massachusetts General Hospital MGH Institute of Health Professions University of Arizona University of Rhode Island Simmons University Hartford Hospital Yale National Clinician Scholars Program New Hampshire Department of Health and Human Services International universities and healthcare organizations Career pathways include: Nurse Scientist University Professor Health Policy Expert Research Director Clinical Researcher Healthcare Executive Public Health Leader Academic Administrator Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Requirements Bachelor s degree or higher in Nursing from a nationally accredited university Completed statistics course Current Registered Nurse (RN) license International Applicants Applicants from outside the United States must possess: A Bachelor of Science in Nursing equivalent to a U.S. BSN degree An active nursing license in their home country Preferred Applicant Qualifications The admissions committee gives preference to applicants who demonstrate: Minimum GPA of 3.5 Strong research experience Clear research interests aligned with faculty expertise Publications and scholarly work Excellent writing ability Leadership experience or leadership potential Application Requirements Applicants must submit: 1. Resume or Curriculum Vitae Include: Academic history Professional experience Nursing licenses Research experience Publications Social justice activities Additional language skills 2. Goal Statement A typed, double-spaced statement of no more than four pages outlining: Educational goals Research interests Professional aspirations 3. Scholarly Writing Sample Examples may include: Academic papers Published articles Training manuals Clinical case analyses Research manuscripts 4. Letters of Recommendation Three recommendation letters are required from: A current or recent employer or professional colleague A professor familiar with your academic potential A professional reference familiar with your clinical practice 5. Academic Transcripts Official transcripts are preferred during application and required upon admission. 6. GRE Scores GRE scores are preferred but not mandatory. English Language Requirements Applicants whose native language is not English must submit evidence of English proficiency. Accepted minimum scores include: TOEFL iBT: 100 IELTS Academic: 7.5 TOEFL Paper-Based Test: 600 TOEFL Computer-Based Test: 250 Certain applicants from English-speaking countries may qualify for exemption. Why Choose Boston College s Nursing Ph.D. Program? The Connell School of Nursing stands out because of: Full financial support World-class faculty mentorship Strong research training Leadership development opportunities Excellent graduate employment outcomes Global academic reputation Diverse and collaborative learning environment Students graduate prepared not only to contribute to nursing science but also to shape healthcare systems and influence policy decisions worldwide. Application Deadline January 15 2027 Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials well in advance of the deadline. APPLY HERE Click HERE to learn more about the Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027. Conclusion The Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring nurse scientists and healthcare leaders seeking rigorous doctoral training in a fully funded environment. Through advanced coursework, intensive research experiences, expert mentorship, and comprehensive financial support, the program prepares graduates to address some of the most pressing healthcare challenges facing communities around the world. For nurses passionate about research, education, policy development, and healthcare innovation, this doctoral program represents a powerful pathway toward meaningful impact and long-term professional success. Discover more global PhD and Scholarship opportunities on OFY. title: Fully Funded PhD in Gender, Peace and Security at the Swedish Defence University 2027 | Salary, Research Funding and International Applicants Welcome
summary: Are you passionate about gender studies, security, peacebuilding, conflict studies, military institutions, and feminist approaches to international security? The Swedish Defence University (SEDU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position in War Studies with a specialization in Gender, Peace and Security. This prestigious doctoral opportunity provides successful candidates with full funding, a competitive monthly salary, research support funding, and the opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research at one of Europe s leading institutions in defence, security, and conflict studies. The position is based in Stockholm, Sweden, and is expected to commence in January 2027. Application Deadline: 24 August 2026 About the Swedish Defence University The Swedish Defence University is an internationally recognized institution specializing in: Defence Studies Security Studies Crisis Management International Security War Studies Political Science Military Science Leadership and Command Studies The university serves as Sweden s premier institution for military and civilian education and research relating to defence, security, conflict, and crisis management. Through its interdisciplinary academic environment, military professionals, policymakers, researchers, and students collaborate to address some of the most pressing security challenges facing societies today. About the Department of War Studies The Department of War Studies is one of the university s largest academic departments, with approximately: 160 staff members 1,000 students and trainees The department conducts internationally recognized research examining: War and armed conflict Military organizations Defence policy Security challenges Crisis management Military capability development Ethics and warfare Gender and security Peace and conflict dynamics War Studies at the Swedish Defence University combines theoretical knowledge with practical applications relevant to contemporary security environments. PhD Specialization: Gender, Peace and Security The Department of War Studies is currently recruiting a doctoral candidate specializing in Gender, Peace and Security. The position focuses on research within the field of feminist security studies and offers considerable flexibility for candidates to develop innovative and impactful research projects. Potential research themes may include: Gender and Military Organizations Research may examine: Gender integration in military institutions Diversity and inclusion within armed forces Leadership and gender dynamics Gendered experiences within military structures Gender and War Preparedness Possible research areas include: Gender dimensions of national defence preparedness Civil preparedness and resilience Security planning and gender inclusion Community resilience during crises and conflict Gender and Warfare Candidates may explore: Gendered impacts of war Military operations and gender perspectives Women’s participation in armed conflict Gender and military strategy Gender and Peace Processes Research topics may include: Women s participation in peace negotiations Gender-inclusive peacebuilding Peace agreements and gender outcomes Post-conflict recovery and reconciliation Feminist Security Studies Applicants may investigate broader questions such as: Gender norms and security governance Feminist approaches to defence policy Gendered understandings of security Critical security studies perspectives The relationship between power, conflict, and gender What the PhD Programme Includes The doctoral programme consists of: Four Years of Fully Funded Doctoral Study The programme comprises: 240 ECTS credits in total 180 ECTS dedicated to doctoral thesis research 60 ECTS of doctoral-level coursework Research Supervision Successful candidates will receive: Dedicated academic supervision Access to experienced researchers Research mentoring Academic development support Teaching Opportunities PhD candidates may also participate in: Teaching activities Academic administration Departmental support activities These duties may account for up to 20% of working time and can extend employment to a maximum of five years. Financial Benefits One of the strongest features of this opportunity is its generous funding package. Monthly Salary Civilian doctoral students receive: Starting salary: SEK 32,600 per month The salary increases throughout the doctoral programme according to the university s salary scale. Annual Research Funding Doctoral candidates receive additional support for: Research travel Academic conferences Literature purchases Research-related expenses Funding available: Up to SEK 30,000 per year Additional Benefits Students also benefit from: Full employment status Professional development opportunities Access to university resources International research networks Collaboration with leading scholars Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet both general and specific doctoral admission requirements. General Requirements Applicants must possess one of the following: A Master’s degree Equivalent qualifications obtained in Sweden or abroad At least 240 ECTS credits, including 60 ECTS credits at postgraduate level Specific Academic Requirements Applicants must have: At least 90 ECTS credits in social sciences or equivalent At least 30 ECTS credits in: War Studies International Relations Security Studies Peace and Conflict Studies Political Science Closely related fields Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Excellent English writing skills Excellent English speaking skills Applications may be submitted in: English Swedish Danish Norwegian Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Academic Excellence Reviewers will assess: Previous academic performance Research achievements Quality of academic writing Research Proposal Quality Strong proposals should demonstrate: Originality Theoretical awareness Methodological clarity Analytical depth Relevance to Gender, Peace and Security Independent Research Capacity Candidates should show: Critical thinking skills Research independence Ability to develop scholarly arguments Additional Merits The following may strengthen an application: Publications Research experience Professional experience Military education Security studies expertise Gender studies background Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out This position offers a rare opportunity to conduct advanced research at the intersection of: Gender Studies Security Studies Peacebuilding International Relations Defence Policy Conflict Studies Students will work within a highly respected academic environment while contributing research that can shape future understanding of security, defence, conflict, and peace processes. The university strongly values diversity and welcomes applicants from different cultural, ethnic, and national backgrounds. Application Deadline Deadline: 24 August 2026 Click here for more and to apply Applications must be submitted before midnight CET/CEST on the closing date. Successful candidates are expected to begin their doctoral studies in January 2027 . For aspiring researchers interested in gender, conflict, peacebuilding, and security studies, this fully funded PhD position offers an exceptional opportunity to pursue impactful research while receiving a competitive salary and extensive research support. Click here for more global scholarship opportunities The post Fully Funded PhD in Gender, Peace and Security at the Swedish Defence University 2027 | Salary, Research Funding and International Applicants Welcome appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship 2026 in Paris, France: Fully Paid Opportunity for Graduate Students
summary: Are you a graduate student passionate about affordable housing, social policy, homelessness prevention, and evidence-based public policy? The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exciting internship opportunity within its Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs (ELS), Social Policy Division, based in Paris, France. This highly competitive internship provides an exceptional opportunity for talented graduate students to contribute to international research, policy development, and data analysis related to affordable housing and homelessness across OECD and European Union countries. The internship is expected to begin in Fall 2026 and will run for a duration of four to six months, offering participants the chance to work alongside leading policy experts while contributing to projects that directly influence housing and social policies worldwide. Application Deadline: 24 June 2026 About the OECD The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organisation consisting of 38 member countries dedicated to promoting policies that improve economic and social well-being around the world. The OECD serves as a global knowledge hub, bringing together governments, policymakers, researchers, and experts to address some of the world s most pressing social, economic, and environmental challenges. Its work spans numerous sectors, including: Economic development Education and skills Employment and labour markets Climate change Digital transformation Healthcare Taxation Governance Innovation Social inclusion Housing policy Through evidence-based research and international cooperation, the OECD develops standards, policy recommendations, and comparative analyses that help countries build stronger and more inclusive societies. About the Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs (ELS) The Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs is the OECD’s central body for promoting inclusive growth and social well-being. Its work focuses on: Creating quality jobs Reducing poverty and social exclusion Strengthening human capital Improving healthcare systems Managing migration effectively Enhancing social protection systems Supporting housing inclusion initiatives Within ELS, the Social Policy Division leads important work on affordable housing, homelessness, housing exclusion, and broader social policy challenges affecting millions of people worldwide. OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship Overview The Social Policy Division is seeking a motivated and talented intern to support ongoing research, analytical work, and policy development relating to: Affordable housing Social housing Homelessness Housing exclusion Housing affordability monitoring Housing-related public policy The successful candidate will work under the supervision of a Senior Policy Analyst and contribute to projects that inform national and international housing policies. Internship Location Paris, France Internship Duration 4 to 6 months Full-time internship Possibility of extension depending on OECD internship policies Preferred Start Date Fall 2026 Key Responsibilities The selected intern will play an important role in supporting the OECD’s housing and homelessness policy agenda. 1. Support Housing Policy Research and Analysis Interns will contribute to the development of research and analytical outputs focused on: Affordable housing challenges Homelessness trends Housing exclusion Country-specific housing policy assessments Policy recommendations for governments This work will help policymakers better understand housing challenges and develop effective solutions. 2. Update the OECD Affordable Housing Database A significant component of the internship involves supporting updates to the OECD Affordable Housing Database. Responsibilities may include: Data collection Data validation Data organisation Quality assurance Comparative analysis of housing indicators Interns will gain practical experience working with internationally recognised datasets used by governments and researchers worldwide. 3. Conduct Comparative Policy Research The intern will perform extensive desk research examining national and local housing policies across OECD and EU countries. Research topics may include: Affordable housing programmes Social housing systems Homelessness prevention strategies Housing support measures Housing affordability indicators Housing market interventions The findings will contribute to OECD reports, policy briefs, and international policy discussions. 4. Support International Expert Workshops The selected candidate will assist in organising an OECD expert workshop in Paris. Tasks may include: Event coordination Research preparation Stakeholder communication Meeting support Documentation and reporting This provides valuable exposure to international policy dialogue and stakeholder engagement. 5. Assist Policymakers and OECD Experts Interns will support broader OECD activities aimed at helping governments: Monitor homelessness trends Assess housing affordability Design housing policies Evaluate policy effectiveness Promote social inclusion Benefits of the OECD Internship The OECD internship offers numerous professional and personal development benefits. Financial Support Interns based in Paris receive: A monthly contribution of €1,000 towards living expenses Leave Entitlement Interns receive: Two and a half working days of annual leave for every full month worked Recognition of French public holidays Flexible Working Arrangements Depending on team requirements, internships may be conducted: In-person Hybrid Fully remote International Exposure Participants gain experience in: International public policy Comparative social policy research Global housing issues International cooperation Multilateral institutions Professional Networking Interns will have opportunities to engage with: OECD experts Government officials Researchers International organisations Policy professionals Member country representatives Eligibility Requirements To be considered for this internship, applicants must meet the following criteria. Academic Requirements Applicants must: Be enrolled in a full-time accredited graduate degree programme throughout the internship period Be pursuing Master s or Doctoral studies or equivalent qualifications Relevant academic disciplines include: Housing Studies Economics Social Policy Political Science Sociology Urban Studies Urban Sociology Public Policy Population Studies Demography Related disciplines Language Requirements Applicants must possess: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English Additional language skills are considered a strong advantage. Technical Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel Strong proficiency in Microsoft Word Quantitative analysis skills Data management capabilities Professional Skills Ideal candidates will have: Strong analytical abilities Excellent drafting skills Research experience Attention to detail Organisational skills Teamwork capabilities Ability to work under tight deadlines Positive attitude and willingness to learn Preferred Experience Experience in any of the following areas will be beneficial: Affordable housing research Homelessness policy analysis Social policy research Public policy analysis Data analysis projects OECD Internship Selection Process The OECD internship recruitment process consists of four stages: Stage 1: Online Application Applicants submit: Application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover letter Stage 2: Pre-Screening Applications are reviewed by the OECD Internship Office and shortlisted for relevant directorates. Stage 3: Interview and/or Written Assessment Selected candidates may be invited to: Attend interviews Complete written assessments Demonstrate relevant competencies Stage 4: Internship Agreement Successful candidates receive an internship offer and complete the required internship agreement. Required Application Documents Applicants should submit: Updated CV Brief cover letter The cover letter should clearly indicate: Nationality Language skills Availability Preferred start date Preferred internship duration Confirmation of enrolment in a graduate programme throughout the internship Why You Should Apply This internship represents an outstanding opportunity for graduate students interested in social policy, housing research, and international development. Participants will gain: Experience working with one of the world s most respected international organisations Exposure to international policymaking Advanced research and analytical experience Networking opportunities with global experts Practical skills in housing policy and homelessness research Experience contributing to evidence-based policy solutions For students aspiring to careers in public policy, international development, research, social affairs, urban planning, or housing policy, this internship provides an exceptional platform for professional growth. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship is 24 June 2026. Click here for more and to apply Interested candidates should carefully review all eligibility requirements and prepare their application materials well before the deadline to maximise their chances of success. title: Lululemon Community Wellbeing Grant 2027: Comprehensive Guide for Global Non‑Profits Advancing Movement & Wellbeing
summary: Overview of the Lululemon Community Wellbeing Grant 2027 The Community Wellbeing Grant , powered by lululemon Gives , is a global annual grant program designed to support community‑led charitable organizations that advance physical, mental, and social wellbeing through movement. Since 2016, lululemon has invested in community resilience by funding organizations that create inclusive access to wellbeing practices. In 2025, lululemon achieved its five‑year goal of investing $75 million USD , impacting over 10 million people worldwide. The company is now committing an additional $100 million to reach 20 million more people by 2030 . The 2027 Community Wellbeing Grant continues this mission by offering financial support, optional wellbeing resources, and a collaborative community for organizations working to remove barriers to wellbeing. Grant Amount and Funding Structure Organizations may apply for up to $50,000 USD , with the following conditions: The requested amount must not exceed 40% of the organization’s most recently completed fiscal year operating budget. Grants are administered through lululemon’s Donor‑Advised Fund at Charities Aid Foundation America (CAFA) . Organizations operating in China Mainland will receive funding directly from lululemon China . The grant is a one‑year program with optional wellbeing support offerings throughout the cycle. Lululemon’s Definition of Wellbeing Lululemon defines wellbeing as a lifetime practice of three interconnected elements: Physical Wellbeing – Feeling empowered to give the body what it needs for health and quality of life. Mental Wellbeing – Feeling emotionally prepared and able to handle the future. Social Wellbeing – Feeling connected, supported, and part of a larger community. Organizations must advance wellbeing through movement programming to qualify. Eligibility Criteria Organizations must meet all of the following: Be a registered non‑profit or charity , or be fiscally sponsored by one. Advance wellbeing by creating equitable access to movement programming . Be community‑led and serve communities experiencing barriers to wellbeing. Operate in one or more of the following countries: Australia, Canada, China Mainland, France, Germany, Hong Kong SAR, India, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Macau SAR, Malaysia, Mexico, Korea, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Singapore, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Thailand, UK, USA, Vietnam. Have an annual budget under $3 million USD (priority consideration). Organizations NOT Eligible Lululemon does not fund: Individuals, religious groups, or third‑party fundraisers Political, legislative, or military‑related initiatives Hospitals, clinics, or health services accepting direct payment Schools, PTAs, libraries, museums, or academic institutions Government entities, private foundations, or registered businesses Organizations funded by lululemon for three consecutive years Organizations with budgets over $10 million USD Organizations with fewer than three board members Organizations operating programming for less than one year Grant Funds Cannot Be Used For One‑time events, retreats, workshops, or sponsorships Regranting or scholarships Lobbying or incentives for government officials Benefits to lululemon or its employees Real estate, endowments, or interest‑bearing accounts Equipment or capital assets totaling $5,000 USD or more Timeline Deadlines Registration Period June 1 – July 24, 2026 (5:00 PM PT) Organizations must complete a registration form to verify eligibility. Eligibility notifications are typically sent within 72 hours . Registration after July 24 disqualifies organizations from applying. Application Period July 6 – July 31, 2026 (5:00 PM PT) Only organizations invited after registration may apply. Early submission is strongly recommended. Review Period August 4 – October 16, 2026 Applications reviewed by lululemon Gives and Grant Review Councils. Evaluation focuses on operational alignment and community impact. Selection Period October 19, 2026 Applicants receive selection notifications. Awards are conditional pending CAFA validation. Documentation CAFA Eligibility Application October 19 – November 13, 2026 Selected organizations must sign a Relationship Agreement. CAFA will request documentation and conduct due diligence. Validation Payment Processing November 16, 2026 – January 27, 2027 Funds Disbursed Cohort Announcement February 2027 Deadline to remember: Registration Deadline: July 24, 2026 Application Deadline: July 31, 2026 How to Apply Complete the Registration Form by July 24, 2026 (5:00 PM PT). Wait for eligibility confirmation (within 72 hours). If eligible, complete the full application between July 6–31, 2026. Prepare required documents for CAFA if selected. Await final validation and funding in February 2027. Lululemon provides a comprehensive FAQ and a 2027 Application Worksheet to support applicants. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more grant opportunities here The post Lululemon Community Wellbeing Grant 2027: Comprehensive Guide for Global Non‑Profits Advancing Movement Wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Audible Career Exploration Grants 2026: Expanding Innovative Career Pathways for Underserved Youth
summary: The Audible Global Center for Urban Innovation has launched the Career Exploration Grants 2026 , a powerful funding opportunity designed to support organizations that are reimagining how underserved youth explore, prepare for, and access meaningful career pathways. This initiative provides $10,000 in grant funding to selected organizations and brings them into a collaborative cohort of five grantees working together to advance career exploration innovation. The program is built for organizations that want to scale existing programs, pilot new ideas, or introduce creative approaches to career readiness. As stated in the document: “Grantees will be awarded $10,000 to support current or new programs and will join a cohort of 5 total recipients for virtual and hybrid convenings to share resources and outcomes.” This article provides a comprehensive overview of the grant, its goals, eligibility requirements, application timeline, and expectations for selected organizations. Program Overview The Career Exploration Grants 2026 aim to support organizations that are committed to expanding access to career exploration for young people ages 16–24. Audible is seeking innovative proposals that introduce new methods, tools, collaborations, or program designs that help youth discover and pursue meaningful career pathways. The program emphasizes creativity and innovation, encouraging applicants to propose approaches that address barriers, engage underserved communities, and introduce new ways of preparing youth for the workforce. What Audible Is Looking For Audible is specifically interested in organizations that demonstrate innovation in one or more of the following areas: Program design New ways to reach or engage a target group New methodologies or mixed‑methods approaches Novel collaborations Solutions to unique social challenges or barriers Use of new tools or technology As the document states: “We are looking to collaborate with organizations that take a unique approach to helping students explore, prepare for, and access meaningful career pathways.” What Selected Organizations Will Gain Organizations selected for the Career Exploration Grants 2026 will receive: $10,000 in funding to support new or existing career exploration programs. Membership in a cohort of five grantees , participating in virtual and hybrid convenings. Opportunities to share learnings, resources, and program outcomes with other innovators. Support from Audible Program Managers through monthly meetings. A platform to showcase program impact during a final hybrid convening. This grant is designed not only to provide funding but also to build a community of changemakers committed to youth career development. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Career Exploration Grants 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be located in or serve youth in Cambridge, MA; Culver City, CA; or Newark, NJ . Must be a high school, college/university, or 501(c)(3) nonprofit . Must serve young people ages 16–24 . Must have a measurement and success evaluation plan . Must be able to produce an impact report summarizing program outcomes. Must focus on innovative career exploration programs . Must run the program during School Year 2026–27 or Summer 2027 . Must attend monthly meetings with Audible Program Managers. Must attend a Final Hybrid Large Group Convening . Must have data protection and security protocols that allow appropriate data sharing. Must own or control rights to any materials submitted in the application. The document confirms: “Applicant is located in, or serves young people living in, Cambridge, MA, Culver City, CA, or Newark, NJ.” Application Timeline Applicants should take note of the following important dates: Applications Open: June 8, 2026 Deadline: June 24, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST Interviews: Late June and Early July Grantees Announced: Mid‑July Deadline: June 24, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST Optional Information Sessions Interested applicants may attend one of the following sessions: June 11th at 11:00 AM EST June 22nd at 3:00 PM EST Grant Distribution Grants will be distributed by Prelude , a national nonprofit offering work‑based learning experiences, and its parent company Array , on behalf of Audible. Terms and Conditions Overview Applicants must agree to Audible’s Terms and Conditions, which include: Compliance with all program requirements Accuracy and completeness of submitted information Proper use of grant funds Confidentiality of non‑public information Restrictions on the use of Audible trademarks and intellectual property Agreement to indemnify Audible under certain circumstances The document states: “Using grant funds solely to support the development or enhancement of career exploration programs as described in its Application.” Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more grant opportunities here The post Audible Career Exploration Grants 2026: Expanding Innovative Career Pathways for Underserved Youth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Accelerating Digital Public Infrastructure with Safeguards 2026: Free ITU and UNDP Global Online Course for Government Officials, Policymakers, ICT Specialists, and Digital Transformation Leaders
summary: As countries around the world accelerate their digital transformation agendas, the need for secure, inclusive, and resilient digital systems has never been greater. Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) is increasingly recognized as a foundational approach for modern governance, service delivery, financial inclusion, and digital innovation. To support governments and digital practitioners in building trusted digital ecosystems, the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) and the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) are offering the Accelerating Digital Public Infrastructure with Safeguards course. This fully funded online training opportunity is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical skills required to implement Digital Public Infrastructure while ensuring citizen protection, privacy, inclusion, and sustainability. Supported through the European Union s Global Gateway Initiative , the course is offered free of charge to selected participants and provides an excellent opportunity for professionals involved in national digital transformation initiatives. About the Accelerating Digital Public Infrastructure with Safeguards Course Digital Public Infrastructure is transforming the way governments provide services and interact with citizens. Built on reusable digital components, interoperable standards, and open architectures, DPI helps countries avoid vendor lock-in while creating scalable systems that support innovation and public service delivery. This intermediate-level course introduces participants to a comprehensive “Safeguards-by-Design” framework , enabling them to build and manage secure, trusted, and citizen-centered digital systems. Participants will gain practical insights into: Digital Public Infrastructure principles and implementation models Digital identity systems Secure data exchange mechanisms Privacy and data protection frameworks Governance and regulatory considerations Sustainable financing approaches Inclusive digital transformation strategies Public sector digital innovation The course focuses on balancing technological innovation with the protection of citizens rights and interests. Course Overview Course Title Accelerating Digital Public Infrastructure with Safeguards Registration Period 18 May 2026 – 28 June 2026 Course Dates 28 July 2026 – 25 August 2026 Location Online (Global Participation) Training Topic Digital Transformation Course Type Online Instructor-Led Training Language English Course Level Intermediate Duration 12 Hours Course Fee Free ($0) for Selected Participants Organizers International Telecommunication Union (ITU) United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Funding Support European Union Global Gateway Initiative Maximum Number of Participants 40 Participants Contact Email ituacademy@itu.int Why Digital Public Infrastructure Matters Digital Public Infrastructure is becoming a key driver of economic growth, public service efficiency, and social inclusion. A robust DPI ecosystem enables governments to: Deliver digital services efficiently Improve access to social protection programs Enhance public sector transparency Facilitate secure digital payments Strengthen digital identity systems Promote innovation across sectors Improve citizen-government interactions Foster financial inclusion Enable data-driven policymaking However, without appropriate safeguards, digital transformation efforts can create risks related to privacy, exclusion, security breaches, and misuse of personal data. This course addresses these challenges by teaching participants how to integrate safeguards into every stage of DPI design and implementation. What Participants Will Learn Upon completing the course, participants will be able to: 1. Understand the Value Proposition of DPI Participants will explore how Digital Public Infrastructure can support national development goals and improve public service delivery. 2. Design Secure and Inclusive Digital Systems The course teaches practical approaches for creating systems that prioritize citizen trust, accessibility, and inclusion. 3. Implement Safeguards-by-Design Principles Participants will learn how to embed privacy, security, and accountability measures into digital infrastructure projects from the outset. 4. Navigate Governance and Regulatory Frameworks The training examines legal and institutional requirements necessary for successful DPI implementation. 5. Develop Sustainable Financing Strategies Participants will understand how to mobilize investments and ensure long-term sustainability of digital infrastructure projects. 6. Manage Digital Identity and Data Exchange Systems The course covers best practices for building interoperable digital identity systems and secure data-sharing frameworks. Practical Learning Through Country Simulations One of the most valuable aspects of this training is its intensive capstone project. Participants will work in teams through realistic country-based simulations where they will: Analyze national digital transformation challenges Balance innovation with citizen protection Design consent-based digital architectures Apply data minimization principles Develop governance frameworks Explore financing mechanisms Create implementation strategies Through these simulations, participants gain hands-on experience that can be directly applied to real-world government digitalization initiatives. Key Topics Covered The curriculum includes: Digital Public Infrastructure fundamentals National digital transformation strategies Digital identity ecosystems Secure and interoperable data exchange Data governance Privacy and consent mechanisms Cybersecurity considerations Citizen protection frameworks Digital inclusion strategies Institutional governance Regulatory frameworks Sustainable financing models Investment mobilization Public sector innovation Benefits of Participating Selected participants will benefit from: Free participation supported by EU funding Training from leading international organizations Exposure to global best practices in digital transformation Practical case studies and simulations Networking with international professionals Enhanced understanding of digital governance Improved technical and policy-making skills Knowledge applicable to national digital transformation projects Professional development in a rapidly growing field Certificate-worthy learning experience from respected institutions Who Should Apply? The course is specifically designed for professionals involved in large-scale digital transformation efforts, including: Government officials Policymakers Legal professionals ICT specialists Digital transformation leaders Civil society representatives Public administration professionals Social protection experts Finance sector officials Business regulatory authorities National digitalization agencies The program is especially relevant for professionals working on: Digital identity initiatives Data interoperability projects Digital payments ecosystems Government digitalization programs Public service modernization efforts Commitment to Inclusion The ITU Academy promotes diversity and inclusion across all its training programs. Applications are welcomed from professionals regardless of: Race Ethnicity Gender Age Religion Economic background Geographic location Special encouragement is given to: Women professionals Participants from developing countries Least Developed Countries (LDCs) Small Island Developing States (SIDS) Landlocked Developing Countries (LLDCs) Important Dates Registration Opens 18 May 2026 Registration Deadline 28 June 2026 Course Period 28 July 2026 – 25 August 2026 How to Apply Interested professionals should submit their registration through the ITU Academy platform before the application deadline. Given that only 40 participants will be selected, early registration is highly recommended. This is a unique opportunity to gain advanced knowledge in Digital Public Infrastructure, digital governance, secure data systems, and inclusive digital transformation while learning directly from experts at ITU and UNDP. title: The AI Explorers Program: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring AI Researchers
summary: The AI Explorers Program is an international research and mentorship initiative created to support high‑potential pre‑doctoral students who aspire to pursue PhD studies in Artificial Intelligence. Designed and led by experts from the University of Michigan and global AI institutions, this program provides a structured pathway for emerging researchers to gain the skills, experience, and scholarly output required to become competitive applicants for top-tier doctoral programs worldwide. This article provides a detailed, broad, and SEO‑optimized overview of the program, its mission, structure, expectations, and opportunities for both mentees and mentors. Program Overview Mission The AI Explorers Program is built around a clear mission: To equip talented pre‑doctoral students with the research experience, mentorship, and academic preparation needed to gain admission into leading PhD programs in AI across the globe. The program achieves this by: Connecting emerging researchers with world‑class mentors in Artificial Intelligence Providing structured research training and academic development Offering a collaborative, international environment that nurtures innovation and scholarly growth Supporting participants through the graduate school application process Program Timeline The AI Explorers Program spans 6–9 months , during which participants engage in: Sustained, mentored research Graduate school preparation Professional development activities This timeline is intentionally designed to allow participants to develop technical depth, produce meaningful research output, and build a strong academic profile. Expected Outputs Participants are expected to complete two major deliverables: 1. Research Output A submission‑ready research paper suitable for: A workshop A conference A journal (depending on the field and scope of the project) 2. Graduate School Application Package Participants will develop: A Statement of Purpose (SOP) A Curriculum Vitae (CV) A Research Statement These materials are crafted with mentor guidance to strengthen the participant’s competitiveness for PhD admissions. Program Structure The AI Explorers Program is built on consistent, high-quality mentorship and community engagement. Its structure includes: 1. Weekly Mentor Meetings Participants meet with their assigned mentors to: Discuss research progress Receive technical guidance Refine methodologies Plan next steps 2. Monthly Cohort Meetings These meetings allow participants to: Present research updates Receive peer feedback Engage in collaborative discussions Build a supportive research community 3. Monthly or Bimonthly ACL Mentorship Sessions These sessions focus on: Research skills Academic writing Career pathways in AI Graduate admissions strategies Program Outcomes By the end of the program, participants will have: Completed a research project under expert mentorship Produced a submission‑ready research paper Developed a polished graduate school application package Gained exposure to global AI research communities Strengthened their academic and professional profile The AI Explorers Program ultimately aims to expand access to advanced study in AI and cultivate the next generation of AI researchers. How to Get Involved Mentee Application Prospective mentees can apply through the program’s official application portal. Mentor Application Experienced researchers and professionals in AI are invited to join as mentors. Partnership and Fundraising Organizations and individuals interested in supporting the program can explore partnership opportunities. Program Organizers The AI Explorers Program is led by distinguished AI researchers: Rada Mihalcea – Director, Michigan Artificial Intelligence Lab; Janice M. Jenkins Collegiate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering Zhijing Jin – Assistant Professor, University of Toronto; Research Scientist, Max Planck Institute; CIFAR AI Chair; ELLIS Advisor Angana Borah – PhD Candidate, Michigan Artificial Intelligence Lab Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post The AI Explorers Program: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring AI Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IBM AI Builders Challenge 2026: Win Up to $15,000, Build AI-Powered Projects, Gain Global Recognition, and Showcase Your Skills to Top Employers
summary: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries, workplaces, and the future of work. As organizations increasingly seek graduates with practical AI skills, students around the world are looking for opportunities to gain hands-on experience, build real-world projects, and demonstrate their capabilities to future employers. To help bridge the growing AI skills gap, IBM has officially launched the IBM AI Builders Challenge 2026, a global innovation competition designed specifically for university students. Through this exciting challenge, participants will gain access to IBM Bob, IBM’s new AI-powered development partner, and apply cutting-edge AI technologies to solve real-world problems while competing for cash prizes, professional networking opportunities, and global recognition. Announced during IBM’s Future of AI in Higher Education Summit in New York City, the challenge is part of IBM’s broader commitment to preparing the next generation of AI-ready professionals and advancing its ambitious goal of helping 30 million people develop new skills by 2030. The challenge also coincides with IBM’s decision to expand free access to IBM Bob to more than 20,000 post-secondary institutions worldwide, creating one of the largest AI learning initiatives available to university students today. What is the IBM AI Builders Challenge? The IBM AI Builders Challenge is a global competition that allows university students to gain practical experience using artificial intelligence throughout the software development lifecycle. Unlike many coding competitions that focus solely on programming, this challenge encourages students to think like real-world developers and innovators by: Designing AI-powered solutions Building functional software projects Solving real-world challenges Collaborating with mentors Demonstrating technical and creative skills Developing portfolio-ready projects The challenge is delivered through IBM SkillsBuild, IBM’s free education and workforce development platform that provides learners with access to digital skills training, certifications, and practical learning opportunities. Why IBM Created the AI Builders Challenge The launch comes at a time when employers increasingly expect graduates to understand and effectively use artificial intelligence in the workplace. According to a survey cited by IBM, many university graduates are still not adequately prepared to use generative AI professionally. While AI technologies continue to reshape industries, students often lack opportunities to gain practical experience before entering the workforce. The AI Builders Challenge seeks to address this gap by providing participants with: Hands-on AI experience Exposure to industry-relevant tools Real-world project development opportunities Professional mentorship Portfolio-building experiences Rather than simply generating code, participants must test, refine, explain, and improve their solutions—mirroring the expectations of modern workplaces. What is IBM Bob? A central component of the challenge is IBM Bob, IBM’s new AI-powered development partner. IBM Bob goes beyond traditional AI coding assistants by supporting developers across the entire software development lifecycle. Key Features of IBM Bob IBM Bob helps developers: Generate code Coordinate development tasks Manage workflows Execute software processes Integrate governance and oversight Scale software systems Modernize applications Unlike earlier AI tools that focused mainly on code generation, IBM Bob supports more comprehensive software development activities, helping users move from isolated coding tasks to coordinated project delivery. For students, this means learning how AI can be applied responsibly and effectively throughout complex development processes. Challenge Themes Participants can choose to build projects under one of several exciting real-world challenge themes. 1. Create with AI: The Future of Creative Industries This theme focuses on how artificial intelligence can transform: Design Media Entertainment Content creation Creative workflows Students are encouraged to explore innovative ways AI can empower creative professionals and industries. 2. Mission Beyond Earth: Space Exploration Participants can develop AI solutions that support: Space research Exploration missions Scientific discovery Aerospace innovation This theme invites students to imagine how AI can contribute to humanity’s future beyond Earth. 3. Work Reimagined: Transforming Industries Through Intelligent Work Systems Students can create AI-powered solutions that improve: Productivity Business processes Workplace collaboration Automation systems Industry transformation Projects can focus on how intelligent systems reshape work across sectors. Benefits of Participating The IBM AI Builders Challenge offers significantly more than prize money. 1. Gain Hands-On AI Experience Participants will: Learn practical AI skills Build real-world solutions Work with advanced AI technologies Develop software engineering expertise 2. Build Portfolio-Ready Projects Students will create projects that can be showcased: On GitHub In professional portfolios During job applications At interviews On LinkedIn profiles These projects can serve as evidence of AI competency for future employers. 3. Access Mentorship and Support Participants receive access to: Expert mentors Technical guidance Office hours Webinars Community support through Discord 4. Expand Professional Networks Students can connect with: Industry professionals Fellow innovators IBM experts Developers Technology leaders 5. Learn Workplace-Relevant Skills Participants develop experience in: AI implementation Software development Problem-solving Project management Collaboration Innovation Prize Pool and Awards IBM is offering a total prize pool of USD 15,000 . Grand Prize The overall winner will receive: USD 5,000 cash prize Recognition as the top project across the entire challenge Monthly Competition Awards The challenge includes two monthly competitions. For each monthly competition: Four winners will be selected Awards range from USD 750 to USD 2,250 Additional Recognition Beyond financial rewards, participants gain valuable exposure within the global technology ecosystem. IBM TechXchange Opportunity One of the most exciting benefits of the challenge is the opportunity for the Grand Prize winner to attend IBM TechXchange . IBM TechXchange is IBM’s flagship global technology conference where: Developers Engineers Researchers Scientists Technology professionals come together to build skills, exchange knowledge, and explore emerging innovations. Participation provides unmatched visibility among technology leaders and employers. Who Can Apply? The challenge is open to: University students College students Higher education learners Applicants must: Be at least 18 years old Meet eligibility requirements in participating countries Importantly, students from all academic disciplines are welcome. You do not need to be studying: Computer Science Software Engineering Information Technology Students from business, arts, sciences, social sciences, engineering, and other fields are encouraged to participate. Individual and Team Participation Students may participate: Individually As part of a team This flexibility allows participants to choose a working style that best suits their strengths and goals. Project Submission Process Participants must: Develop a project using IBM Bob. Build a solution aligned with one of the challenge themes. Submit their final project through GitHub. Demonstrate innovation, technical quality, and feasibility. Judging Criteria Projects will be evaluated by a panel of judges based on: Technical execution Innovation Alignment with challenge themes Quality of implementation Practical feasibility The judging process emphasizes both creativity and real-world applicability. Key Dates Registration Opens June 3, 2026 First Competition Launch July 1, 2026 First Competition Deadline July 31, 2026 Second Competition Launch August 1, 2026 Second Competition Deadline August 31, 2026 Students are encouraged to register early to maximize preparation time. Learn More HERE IBM SkillsBuild: Supporting the Future Workforce The AI Builders Challenge is part of IBM SkillsBuild, a global initiative that provides free access to: AI learning pathways Digital credentials Hands-on labs Professional certifications Career-focused training The program reflects IBM’s commitment to helping students move beyond AI literacy toward true AI fluency. Why Students Should Apply The AI Builders Challenge provides an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience in one of the fastest-growing areas of technology. Participants will: Develop AI and software development skills. Build impressive portfolio projects. Gain mentorship from experts. Compete for cash prizes. Expand professional networks. Increase employability. Engage with real-world innovation challenges. As AI continues to transform industries globally, students who gain practical experience today will be better positioned to thrive in tomorrow’s workforce. Participation is subject to the eligibility criteria and other terms in the Official Rules . Click HERE to learn more and apply for the IBM AI Builders Challenge 2026. Conclusion The IBM AI Builders Challenge 2026 represents one of the most exciting global opportunities for university students interested in artificial intelligence, innovation, and software development. By combining hands-on learning, real-world problem-solving, mentorship, professional networking, and substantial cash prizes, the challenge equips participants with valuable skills that employers increasingly demand. Whether you are an aspiring developer, entrepreneur, researcher, designer, or innovator, this competition provides a powerful platform to demonstrate your talents, build career-ready projects, and become part of the next generation of AI-powered problem solvers. Discover more global Competition and AI opportunities on OFY. The post IBM AI Builders Challenge 2026: Win Up to $15,000, Build AI-Powered Projects, Gain Global Recognition, and Showcase Your Skills to Top Employers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NFLPA Fall 2026–Spring 2027 Internship Program: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Sports Industry Professionals
summary: The NFL Players Association (NFLPA) has officially opened applications for its Fall 2026–Spring 2027 Internship Program , offering one of the most immersive and respected training experiences for students and recent graduates seeking careers in sports, business, communications, law, wellness, events, marketing, and player services. Deadline: June 23, 2026 This article provides a broad, detailed, SEO‑optimized overview of the program, its goals, requirements, departmental roles, responsibilities, and expectations—ensuring applicants understand every aspect before applying. Overview of the NFLPA Internship Program According to the NFLPA, “The goal of the NFLPA’s internship program is to assist colleges and universities that allow internships by providing on-the-job training to aid in career preparation and provide relevant professional experience to students and recent graduates interested in working in the sports industry.” The program is structured to expose interns to real-world operations inside one of the most influential labor organizations in professional sports. Interns work directly with staff, contribute to high-impact projects, and gain hands-on experience across multiple departments. Application Window Opens: June 9, 2026 Closes: June 23, 2026 Applicants must submit all required materials before the deadline to be considered. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be entering junior or senior year of an undergraduate program, or be enrolled in a graduate program or law school. Doctoral students are not eligible. Must have a minimum GPA of 3.0 . Recent graduates must have completed their degree within one year of applying. Must submit: Cover letter with full contact information and up to two preferred departments Resume Recent transcript (unofficial accepted) Three references with full contact details One essay response (within character limit) to one of the following: Essay 1: Should professional athletes behave as role models? Essay 2: Do athletes have a responsibility to speak on social justice issues? Hours, Compensation Housing The NFLPA outlines the following: Work hours: 27.5 hours per week Stipend: $18 per hour Commuting costs: Covered Housing: Not provided Parking: Not provided Housing resources: External links provided for informational purposes only (e.g., ApartmentList, WIHN, 4Stay, university housing options) DEPARTMENT-BY-DEPARTMENT INTERNSHIP DETAILS Below is a full breakdown of every department and its responsibilities, as described in the document. NFL Players Association Departments 1. External Affairs / Communications Interns support messaging, media monitoring, content creation, and digital strategy. Key Responsibilities: Weekly media monitoring and tracking NFLPA mentions Managing media inbox inquiries Supporting editorial and campaign planning Creating videos, visuals, and social media copy Writing press releases and updating website content Researching creative/media partnership opportunities Assisting with internal communications and project management 2. Player Affairs: Wellness Interns support mental health, wellness resources, clinician directories, and recovery initiatives. Key Responsibilities: Maintain Clinician Directory database Assist with clinician onboarding and conference planning Identify wellness resources for players Monitor mental health trends Support wellness campaigns and newsletters Manage wellness inventory and inbox Track injury trends with Data Analytics Participate in Wellness Committee 3. Player Affairs (General PAD) Interns support programming, data analysis, events, and player engagement. Key Responsibilities: Assist in program development and evaluation Track and analyze program data Maintain databases and surveys Prepare presentations and materials Support events and outreach Manage content inventory Coordinate with vendors Conduct industry research Support initiatives in career development, finance, education, and more 4. Player Affairs: Former Player Services Interns support data integrity, social media, content creation, and player engagement. Key Responsibilities: Update PA.Net data Research missing player information Contact players for updated details Create social media graphics and campaigns Conduct interviews with former player reps Manage birthday posts and feature articles Support Financial Advisor Program Assist with chapter audits and heat maps Verify membership for events and rewards Audit discount programs and promo codes 5. Events Travel Interns support planning and execution of major NFLPA events. Key Responsibilities: Assist with Super Bowl, NFL Draft Week, Rookie Premiere, and more Conduct venue research and site visits Support travel logistics Develop event collateral Organize local events Manage event shipments and inventory Provide administrative support 6. Salary Cap Agent Administration Interns gain exposure to contract management, salary cap analysis, and agent relations. Key Responsibilities: Update signing bonus deferral charts Upload and reconcile contracts Maintain contract language files Support agent seminars and fee trend analysis Assist with starter reports and compensation analyses Support unrestricted free agency reports NFL Players Inc. Departments 7. Consumer Products Strategy (Licensing) Interns support product development, licensing, data analysis, and brand identity. Key Responsibilities: Manage relationships with licensees Support product development and approvals Monitor distribution channels and social media Maintain electronic filing systems Assist with P L and budget projections Analyze datasets for trends Develop dashboards and reports Brainstorm new product ideas 8. Partner Services Interns support sponsor relationships, marketing programs, and contract deliverables. Key Responsibilities: Manage partner relationships Track deliverables and approvals Coordinate creative asset approvals Conduct market and brand research Prepare presentations and reports Support player marketing activations Collaborate across departments 9. Player Services Interns support player marketing, logistics, data management, and administrative tasks. Key Responsibilities: Assist players, agents, and partners Update contract templates Manage transportation logistics Enter deal information into CRM Research player interests and backgrounds Support MRN and W‑9 processes Assist with marketing case studies 10. Commercial Strategy Legal Interns support revenue strategy, business development, legal research, and contract drafting. Key Responsibilities (Commercial Strategy): Maintain business development resources Create and distribute departmental materials Participate in interdepartmental meetings Conduct research on trends and opportunities Support event logistics Provide administrative support Key Responsibilities (Legal – for rising 3Ls): Research IP, rights of publicity, and legal issues Draft memoranda and agreements Support compliance and governance Assist with brand protection Work closely with senior legal staff The Trust (Powered by the NFLPA) 11. Communications, Engagement Marketing (CME) Interns support storytelling, social media, content creation, and event communications. Key Responsibilities: Manage Trust Player Spotlight series Plan and schedule social media content Support event communications Collect and organize media assets Monitor media and social mentions Contribute to marketing presentations 12. Service Team Interns support client services, CRM management, administrative tasks, and player communication. Key Responsibilities: Provide players with information on Trust benefits Manage a small caseload Support administrative tasks and data entry Maintain resource guides Organize CRM database Assist with meeting coordination Track performance metrics Conduct research on best practices Conclusion The NFLPA Fall 2026–Spring 2027 Internship Program is one of the most comprehensive and hands-on opportunities available to students and recent graduates seeking careers in sports, business, law, communications, wellness, events, and player services. With multiple departments offering specialized training, interns gain unmatched exposure to the inner workings of a major professional sports union. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more internship opportunities here The post NFLPA Fall 2026–Spring 2027 Internship Program: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Sports Industry Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
